content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder (App/Info.plist), and NSCameraUsageDescription ("This app needs camera access.") is generic and doesn't mention its actual use (document scanning, per ScannerView.swift's "Point the camera at a document").
Pierre: Apple reviewers reject apps with placeholder or vague purpose strings because they don't let users make an informed consent decision, and a literal "TODO" is an obvious giveaway of unfinished work. Also, there's no visible feature in the code that uses location at all, so it's unclear why that permission is requested. Fix the camera string to reference document scanning specifically (e.g. "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents"), write a real justification for location or remove the key if unused.
